contractBase.rationale: Existing Coco docs/design line for character visual locks, canonical import gates, asset readiness, and shop-card presentation; its changed paths contain no src or runtime files.

runtimeReferences.usage: read-only Arena Ruins color/readability and board-scale baseline
read-only asset-animation and motion-pipeline technical baseline

ancestryBoundary.rule: Do not merge, rebase, or blind-cherry-pick either CC runtime ancestry into this Coco contract. Future integration consumes this contract and approved binaries path-limited.

primaryReference.direction: Cute stylized fantasy chibi, gameplay-first readability
primaryReference.approvalIntent: The style and presentation should closely follow this reference; the archived reference itself is not a production or runtime asset.

lockedTraits: rock mass remains readable when reduced

required: hands and class-defining weapons remain readable
forbidden: super-deformed baby proportions that erase class authority
micro-detail that disappears at board scale
one repeated face differentiated only by hair or costume

silhouetteRules.recognitionTarget: Class, role, or monster archetype should be identifiable within approximately one second.
priority: headgear, hair, hood, horns, ears, or crystals
forbidden: repeated cloak/costume blockout across class lines
weapon silhouettes hidden behind the body

colorSeparation: one clear dominant class or material color
retain foreground separation against Arena Ruins warm sand and stone
readabilityRules.acceptance: A production candidate must pass side-by-side review in all four contexts; decorative detail cannot compensate for a failed silhouette or face read.

required: hand-painted or softly rendered material feel
crisp readable outer and internal edges
transparent character export without card or scene pixels
forbidden: baked card frame, text, price, rarity border, scene, floor, or board background
baked floor shadow that contains scene or board-specific pixels

heroes: unique head, hair, hood, helmet, or ear shape
do not repeat cloak shape and costume blockout between classes
monsters: shape language identifies the archetype immediately
ranged monsters show a readable ranged weapon or casting cue
bosses gain visual authority through scale, mass, framing, and focal detail without abandoning chibi language

genderConstraints.descendantGenderRule: Archer and Acolyte Class 2 descendants remain female.
genderConstraints.otherClassLines: Retain the existing hero-codex-v1 gender records unless a separately approved design contract changes them.

boardCompatibility.background: warm sand and stone Arena Ruins
required: Slime blue hue and glossy highlights must not blend into floor accents
Golem crystals, eye glow, massing, and edge values must prevent stone-on-stone loss
Archer and human classes need readable head, weapon, and dominant-color separation
forbidden: baked Arena Ruins background or floor pixels
baked board tile, bench, perimeter, prop, or map-specific pixels
changing camera, board dimensions, or gameplay geometry to compensate for weak character readability

meaning.styleDirectionApprovedMeaning: The user approved the project-wide visual language and the four logical references.
meaning.canonicalApprovedMeaning: False because no newly migrated production character binary is approved by this contract.

exactFileGate: no baked background, floor, shadow, UI, text, or card pixels
neutral master matches the style lock
board, bench, shop-card, and enemy-list readability passed
user explicitly approves that exact production file

technicallyReusable.reuse: asset resolution, animation state machine, FPS sanitization, fallback, texture cache/ref-count disposal, and marker delivery
state selection, x1/x4 playback, pause/restart, flipX, transition sequencing, diagnostics, and dispose lifecycle
sidecar/source-map shape, unit/state identity, FPS, frame count, loop, anchor, event-marker, root-motion, and provenance fields; values must be revalidated for a changed visual master
test and validation infrastructure, with future expected hashes and visual-master-specific assertions updated only in the replacement asset task

visuallyRequiresReplacement.reason: Current Archer is closer to realistic adult proportions and does not match the approved green-haired, large-eyed, very-chibi anchor. All states must be regenerated after a new exact-file neutral master is approved.
Current Golem shares stone/crystal cues but has a taller heroic humanoid mass; the approved anchor requires a more compact chunky body and stronger oversized-fist chibi read.
Legacy/procedural Class 1, Class 2, portrait, and composited sheets do not meet the new polished stylized-3D chibi language and cannot become final migrated visuals.

Legacy monster and boss sprites predate this style lock; each active roster entry needs a new style-conforming neutral master and exact-file review. Obsolete roster art is not migrated unless separately reactivated.

possiblyReusableWithReview.reason: The current bright-blue glossy silhouette and small horns are directionally close, but the approved anchor has a rounder, friendlier face with very large shiny eyes and a cute smile. Reuse requires exact reduced-size comparison and explicit approval; otherwise regenerate all states from the new Slime master.

technicalReusePolicy.baseline: PR #56 is technically reusable as a read-only motion/runtime baseline.
allowed: reuse animation state and transition architecture
reuse sidecar schema and marker vocabulary
technicalReusePolicy.condition: Reusing a system or schema does not approve any old visual frame.

visualReplacementPolicy.rule: When a neutral visual master changes silhouette, proportion, costume, face, weapon, or material substantially, every animation and portrait derived from the old master becomes replacement-planned rather than final.
visualReplacementPolicy.preservation: Do not delete, overwrite, recolor, retouch, or regenerate existing assets in this contract task.
visualReplacementPolicy.promotion: New files remain canonicalApproved=false until separate exact-file approval.

requiredAfterMasterApproval: derive every state from the same approved neutral master
regenerate all old-master states affected by silhouette or proportion change
revalidate canvas, anchor, baseline, crop, flip, FPS, loop, root motion, and event markers
retain gameplay marker timing semantics unless a separate gameplay-authorized task changes them
run package transitions and x4 playback after integration
motionMigrationRule.prohibition: Do not treat technically passing PR #56 frames as final visuals after their source master is replaced.

migrationPhases.name: Style Anchors
migrationPhases.sourceArt: Use the three logical style anchors plus the primary gameplay context; create new production source in a later task, never by extracting reference pixels.
migrationPhases.neutralIdleMaster: Approve one transparent neutral full-body master per anchor before any state production. Archer defines hero language, Slime simple-monster language, and Golem heavy-monster language.
migrationPhases.motionRegenerationRequirement: Archer and Golem require replacement motion sets. Slime requires exact comparison first and regeneration if it misses the approved face/proportion lock.
migrationPhases.cardPortraitRequirement: Produce board/bench sprite, shop-card presentation, and enemy-list portrait crops from the same approved master without baking UI into source art.
migrationPhases.runtimeIntegrationImpact: Path-limited asset/sidecar replacement through existing PR #56 seams; no Core Logic, Combat, camera, board, or map change.
migrationPhases.approvalGate: Each neutral master and each derived production set receives separate exact-file approval; style approval alone is insufficient.

migrationPhases.name: Class 1 Hero Set
migrationPhases.sourceArt: New style-conforming full-body source for all seven classes, preserving the existing class/gender/weapon identity contracts.
migrationPhases.neutralIdleMaster: One approved neutral/idle master per class with unique face, head shape, weapon cue, dominant color, and silhouette.
migrationPhases.motionRegenerationRequirement: Generate required idle/move/attack and later skill/hit/death states only after the class master is approved.
migrationPhases.cardPortraitRequirement: Shop-card and portrait/icon views must remain identifiable and consistent with the board sprite.
migrationPhases.runtimeIntegrationImpact: Reuse current loaders and state contracts; replace visuals path-limited after asset approval.
migrationPhases.approvalGate: Class-by-class exact-file gate plus one seven-class contact-sheet differentiation review.

migrationPhases.name: Core Monsters
migrationPhases.sourceArt: New monster masters following simple/heavy/ranged/assassin shape-language rules and the active Map 1 roster IDs.
migrationPhases.neutralIdleMaster: One approved master per monster; ranged and heavy archetypes must read without color alone.
migrationPhases.motionRegenerationRequirement: Regenerate all states whose old master is replaced; preserve authorized gameplay markers and root-motion semantics.
migrationPhases.cardPortraitRequirement: Enemy-list portrait/icon and board sprite must share identity and remain distinct against Arena Ruins.
migrationPhases.runtimeIntegrationImpact: Visual-only path-limited integration through existing seams; map encounter data remains unchanged.
migrationPhases.approvalGate: Per-monster exact-file gate plus full active-monster silhouette strip at reduced size.

migrationPhases.name: Class 2
migrationPhases.sourceArt: New evolutions derived from their approved Class 1 family language while remaining unmistakable from the parent and sibling branch.
migrationPhases.neutralIdleMaster: Approve parent/sibling comparison before animation; Archer and Acolyte descendants remain female.
migrationPhases.motionRegenerationRequirement: No legacy Class 2 sheet is final; build states from each approved Class 2 master.
migrationPhases.cardPortraitRequirement: Evolution branch remains readable in shop/card/portrait scale without relying on labels.
migrationPhases.runtimeIntegrationImpact: Reuse class IDs and technical asset framework; no fusion or gameplay rule change.
migrationPhases.approvalGate: Parent-plus-two-branch exact-file and silhouette comparison per class line.

migrationPhases.name: Bosses
migrationPhases.sourceArt: New chibi-fantasy boss masters with larger authority and focal detail while staying readable and compatible with the same rendering language.
migrationPhases.neutralIdleMaster: Approve scale, mass, focal feature, and reduced-size portrait before motion.
migrationPhases.motionRegenerationRequirement: Regenerate active boss motion from approved masters; do not migrate obsolete warden/champion art unless gameplay separately reactivates those IDs.
migrationPhases.cardPortraitRequirement: Enemy/boss list portrait and board-scale body need a consistent focal read.
migrationPhases.runtimeIntegrationImpact: Asset-only replacement; boss stats, skills, encounter ownership, targeting, and stage logic remain protected.
migrationPhases.approvalGate: Exact-file boss master approval plus board-scale readability and effect-clearance review.

migrationPhases.name: Secret Classes
migrationPhases.sourceArt: Create only from separately approved secret-class briefs. Reserved Map 2/3 IDs remain unproduced until canonical data exists.
migrationPhases.neutralIdleMaster: Approve secret-class silhouette and differentiation from all normal/evolved classes before motion.
migrationPhases.motionRegenerationRequirement: Create states only after the exact master and production scope are approved.
migrationPhases.cardPortraitRequirement: Secret rarity/authority is expressed by character design; UI frames remain separate assets.
migrationPhases.runtimeIntegrationImpact: Future visual integration only; unlock, save, shop, fusion, Combat, and Map 2/3 behavior remain untouched.
migrationPhases.approvalGate: Separate brief approval, exact-file gate, differentiation review, and explicit runtime task.

protectedAreas: map runtime and Arena Ruins production assets
existing motion and character production assets
futureValidationGates: no baked background, floor, shadow, UI, text, or card pixels
board, bench, shop-card, and enemy-list readability
frame identity, baseline, crop, loop, root motion, markers, and x4 regression
